channel.title: 音楽ニュースリスト - Bluesky Feed
channel.description: 音楽ニュースサイトの発信をまとめて確認するためのリスト

item.title: The move makes France the first country in the European Unio…
item.author: NPR (@npr.org)
item.description: The move makes France the first country in the European Union to pass a blanket ban on platforms as concerns grow worldwide over the harmful effects of digital content on kids. n.pr/3TMwz94
item.content:encoded: The move makes France the first country in the European Union to pass a blanket ban on platforms as concerns grow worldwide over the harmful effects of digital content on kids. n.pr/3TMwz94

item.title: About 24% of U.S. employees stay in unwanted jobs just for h…
item.author: NPR (@npr.org)
item.description: About 24% of U.S. employees stay in unwanted jobs just for health insurance, a report out Wednesday finds. Up significantly since 2021, the uptick in "job lock" is a concerning sign for the U.S. economy. n.pr/4gQIY5E
item.content:encoded: About 24% of U.S. employees stay in unwanted jobs just for health insurance, a report out Wednesday finds. Up significantly since 2021, the uptick in "job lock" is a concerning sign for the U.S. economy. n.pr/4gQIY5E

item.title: Lawmakers are hoping to avoid a third shutdown since last Se…
item.author: NPR (@npr.org)
item.description: Lawmakers are hoping to avoid a third shutdown since last September. The stopgap funding measure now heads to the Senate, where it faces an uncertain future. n.pr/4wbhRqx
item.content:encoded: Lawmakers are hoping to avoid a third shutdown since last September. The stopgap funding measure now heads to the Senate, where it faces an uncertain future. n.pr/4wbhRqx

item.description:

小さな心の動きや人生の転機歌った新曲配信
https://natalie.mu/music/news/681863?utm_source=bluesky&utm_medium=social

レコーディングには
・増村和彦（Dr / ex. 森は生きている）
・谷口雄（Piano / ex. 森は生きている、1983）
・サボテン楽団（G）
・中川理沙（Cho）
が参加

#ポニーのヒサミツ

item.title: Presenting The Rolling Stone Interview with @zohrankmamdani.…
item.author: Rolling Stone (@rollingstone.com)
item.description:
Presenting The Rolling Stone Interview with @zohrankmamdani.bsky.social

The NYC mayor talks about the future of the Democratic Party, what he's learned over his first six months in office, dealing with President Trump, Taylor Swift's wedding, and more.

Read ↓ bit.ly/45fGwyp

item.title: When Leslie Buck returned home after escaping her kidnapper,…
item.author: Rolling Stone (@rollingstone.com)
item.description:
When Leslie Buck returned home after escaping her kidnapper, she burst through the door. Instead of falling into her husband’s arms, though, she yelled, “I hate you and I hate Russell Kirby!”

Read more of the story on Leslie Buck’s tragic end. ⤵
apple.news/At4X8fv9wQAG...
item.content:encoded:
When Leslie Buck returned home after escaping her kidnapper, she burst through the door. Instead of falling into her husband’s arms, though, she yelled, “I hate you and I hate Russell Kirby!”

Read more of the story on Leslie Buck’s tragic end. ⤵
apple.news/At4X8fv9wQAG...

item.title: In a House DOGE subcommittee hearing, GOP representatives ex…
item.author: NPR (@npr.org)
item.description: In a House DOGE subcommittee hearing, GOP representatives excoriated the director of the National Museum of American History, following a White House report earlier this month that also criticized her. n.pr/44GtxWs
item.content:encoded: In a House DOGE subcommittee hearing, GOP representatives excoriated the director of the National Museum of American History, following a White House report earlier this month that also criticized her. n.pr/44GtxWs

item.title: “I’ve been haunted by what happened for decades—by [my teach…
item.author: Rolling Stone (@rollingstone.com)
item.description:
“I’ve been haunted by what happened for decades—by [my teacher’s] death, yes, but also what a dramatic end does to a person. How it becomes entertainment,” @brennaehrlich.bsky.social writes.

Her exploration of Leslie Buck and how a murder becomes true crime: apple.news/At4X8fv9wQAGcCsz7GDauPQ
item.content:encoded:
“I’ve been haunted by what happened for decades—by [my teacher’s] death, yes, but also what a dramatic end does to a person. How it becomes entertainment,” @brennaehrlich.bsky.social writes.

Her exploration of Leslie Buck and how a murder becomes true crime: apple.news/At4X8fv9wQAGcCsz7GDauPQ

item.title: Russell Kirby, a friend of Leslie Buck’s husband, kidnapped …
item.author: Rolling Stone (@rollingstone.com)
item.description:
Russell Kirby, a friend of Leslie Buck’s husband, kidnapped her in 2002. He hit her, tackled her, bound her, and forced her into her own car.

She escaped and came home. The next day, Leslie was dead.

Read Rolling Stone’s investigation into her death: apple.news/At4X8fv9wQAG...
item.content:encoded:
Russell Kirby, a friend of Leslie Buck’s husband, kidnapped her in 2002. He hit her, tackled her, bound her, and forced her into her own car.

She escaped and came home. The next day, Leslie was dead.

Read Rolling Stone’s investigation into her death: apple.news/At4X8fv9wQAG...

item.title: Rolling Stone Reports, only on Apple News: Was My Teacher Mu…
item.author: Rolling Stone (@rollingstone.com)
item.description:
Rolling Stone Reports, only on Apple News: Was My Teacher Murdered?

In 2002, Leslie Buck died under mysterious circumstances in Mystic, Connecticut. Two decades later, @brennaehrlich.bsky.social went back to find out who she was — and what really happened.

Read: apple.news/At4X8fv9wQAG...
item.content:encoded:
Rolling Stone Reports, only on Apple News: Was My Teacher Murdered?

In 2002, Leslie Buck died under mysterious circumstances in Mystic, Connecticut. Two decades later, @brennaehrlich.bsky.social went back to find out who she was — and what really happened.

Read: apple.news/At4X8fv9wQAG...

item.title: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy fired Gen. Oleksandr Syrskyi a…
item.author: NPR (@npr.org)
item.description: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy fired Gen. Oleksandr Syrskyi as commander-in-chief of Ukraine's armed forces following days of protests demanding his removal. Zelenskyy appointed Mykhailo Drapatyi as the new army chief. n.pr/4gQvhU9
item.content:encoded: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy fired Gen. Oleksandr Syrskyi as commander-in-chief of Ukraine's armed forces following days of protests demanding his removal. Zelenskyy appointed Mykhailo Drapatyi as the new army chief. n.pr/4gQvhU9

item.title: Self Defense Family will also play some rare live shows over…
item.author: Stereogum (@stereogum.com)
item.description:
Self Defense Family will also play some rare live shows over the next month — a pair with their peers in Fiddlehead and one in Chicago with reunited Philadelphia heroes Ink & Dagger.

stereogum.com/2505846/self...
item.content:encoded:
Self Defense Family will also play some rare live shows over the next month — a pair with their peers in Fiddlehead and one in Chicago with reunited Philadelphia heroes Ink & Dagger.

item.title: Today, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth told senators that ins…
item.author: Rolling Stone (@rollingstone.com)
item.description:
Today, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth told senators that instead of criticism, we should be praising Trump for dragging us into war.

Read: www.rollingstone.com/politics/pol...
item.content:encoded:
